title: The Cobblers' Catch.
content:
Come sit we by the fire's side,
    And roundly drink we here;
    Till that we see our cheeks ale-dy'd
    And noses tann'd with beer.
meaning:
Robert Herrick's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Cobblers' Catch."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
